frozen_components: LLM backbone (2B–7B, frozen during safety re-injection)
architecture.summary: Holographic Invariant Storage assembles properties of bipolar Vector Symbolic Architectures (VSA) into a design-time safety contract for LLM context-drift mitigation. The system uses closed-form mathematical guarantees about signal recovery, noise robustness, and multi-signal capacity to enable pre-deployment budgeting of recovery fidelity and codebook capacity. A VSA encoding binds safety properties into distributed vector representations that can be injected into LLM context and recovered with quantified fidelity bounds, crossing the model boundary via text or activation-space injection of safety constraints.

biblio.title: Holographic Invariant Storage: Design-Time Safety Contracts via Vector Symbolic Architectures

claims.statement: single-signal recovery fidelity converges to 1/√2 ≈ 0.707 regardless of noise depth or content
closed-form guarantees validated by Monte Carlo simulation
safety re-injection improves adherence at the 2B scale
pilot behavioral experiment across four LLMs (2B–7B, 720 trials) confirms safety re-injection efficacy with full results in appendix
HIS enables systems engineers to budget recovery fidelity and codebook capacity at design time
design-time safety contracts provide property no timer or embedding-distance metric provides
